input:
His credibility, however, was eroded. Whenever he said or did something, his own people were the first to take issue with him and remonstrate with him, trying to make him see his error. When this was apparent, the Prophet said to ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb, the first to suggest that ʿAbdullāh ibn Ubayy should be killed: “Now do you see, ʿUmar? Had I killed him when you suggested that to me, some people would have been very angry, while they themselves would be prepared to kill him now if I would only order them to do so.” ʿUmar replied: “I certainly know that God’s Messenger knows better than I do and his actions are more blessed than mine.” The Wicked Designs of Hypocrisy A brief reference has already been made to another incident which the hypocrites tried to exploit to the detriment of the Muslims, during the expedition of al-Muṣṭalaq.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 24: How to Mar a Splendid Victory

output.scene_title: The Prophet's Wisdom and ʿUmar's Realization
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 24: How to Mar a Splendid Victory

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: ʿUmar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb
characters.appearance: Late 30s, dark olive skin, athletic build, strong facial features. Thick black beard, neatly trimmed. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Confident and serious expression.

characters.name: ʿAbdullāh ibn Ubayy
characters.appearance: Late 40s, light brown skin, slightly overweight, weak facial features. Thin grey beard, poorly kept. Wearing worn-out off-white thobe, dark cloak, simple headscarf. Sneering, condescending expression, shown from behind.

output.moral_lesson: True wisdom lies in recognizing one's limitations and trusting in others' knowledge and guidance.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet pointed out to ʿUmar that if he had killed ʿAbdullāh ibn Ubayy, some people would have been angry. But now, they themselves want to kill him. ʿUmar humbly admitted that the Prophet knows better than he does.

compliance.notes: The scene shows the Prophet from behind, ʿUmar with full face, and ʿAbdullāh ibn Ubayy from behind. The atmosphere is calm and educational.

output.image_prompt: Children's illustration in a traditional Islamic art style. A spacious 7th century Makkah home with high ceilings and large windows. The Prophet stands at the center, seen entirely from behind, wearing a green cloak over a white thobe, with a white turban and simple leather sandals. ʿUmar, wearing off-white thobe and brown cloak, sits on a woven mat, looking up at the Prophet with a respectful expression. ʿAbdullāh ibn Ubayy, dressed in a worn-out off-white thobe and dark cloak, stands in the shadows, shown from behind, with a sneering expression. Soft, natural lighting. Warm beige palette. No modern objects.

metadata.chapter_title: How to Mar a Splendid Victory
